mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the effective lower bound and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, the equity discount-rate channel dominates: persistently low policy rates support higher S&P 500 prices over the next month by lowering the present value of cash flows.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if higher FEDFUNDS within the low-rate regime is associated with equal or better forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE over short windows signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces expected earnings and increases recession risk; that combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the average forward return is nonnegative across triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combined upturn in RSAFS and HOUST identifies strengthening final demand and housing-sensitive spending, which lifts revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not positive on average over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and operating leverage, which should raise cyclical earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than half of occurrences, or if the sign of the average response is not positive after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in GDPC1 growth indicates economy-wide demand deceleration that eventually compresses earnings revisions and lowers equity prices; the effect is strongest on a quarterly horizon after the slowdown becomes visible.
failure_condition: Reject if low-GDPC1-growth periods are not associated with negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the forward return distribution is indistinguishable from unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, payroll gains and tightening labor conditions imply accelerating income flows and improved risk appetite, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down configuration fails to outperform either component alone, or if triggered episodes do not yield positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, FEDFUNDS above neutral-like levels raises discount rates and signals restrictive policy that should depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-period observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show negative 5-day SPX returns on average, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST confirms demand strength in both consumption and housing, and that breadth of demand should translate into positive equity performance over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint monthly increases in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the combined signal loses predictive power after controlling for each series alone.
